review_title: Detailed User Insights
description: It gives valuable insights into user behaviour, but it’s best for those willing to learn the ropes.
author: Ava H.
pros: I tracked what users were doing, helping me see what they enjoyed and where they struggled.
cons: It’s tricky to learn at first, especially if you’re new to analytics.

review_title: Mixpanel is a must go for every app. Easy to start with.
description: Overall Mixpanel is a great tool. 1. Tracks users from apps. 2. Track user journeys. 3. Advanced reports. 4. Easy to integrate with backend to pull in events.
author: Lovepreet S.
pros: 1. Easy to integrate. 2. Identity merge is great for people using same device with multiple users. 3. Accurately tracks user journeys, phone properties etc. 4. Reports are easy to make.
cons: 1. Sometimes due to technical issues, multiple users get created despite identity merge being enabled. 2. They keep changing there free plan limit and overall plan is quite costly for paid feature.

review_title: Mixpanel enables scalable, self-service analytics across Product, UX, and Data
author: Flavio M.
pros: Mixpanel is quite useful when we want to track user engagement and activity, also sending pipeline and warehouse event data for the datawarehouse team have visibility and do analysis, also is goof to avoid duplication of data. Mixpanel enables scalable, self-service analytics across Product, UX, and Data teams—supporting faster, evidence-based decision-making. It also allows us to measure the impact of product changes and experiments.
cons: Do not noticed any bigger issue with the tool, but sometimes it was a bit difficult to understand when started using it. So learning curve could be a bit higher for beginners.

review_title: Great product for preliminary analysis
author: Nitin M.
pros: It's great for preliminary analysis. The UI is super neat and any person can get started with little-to-no setup
cons: In-depth analysis is a little tough. Exporting data is also tricky

review_title: Great Analytics tool for Decision Making
description: It was a great product we use it on daily bases to track the user activity on our site and based on the research we plan our future goals.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: 1. It is highly useful for those products which are based on customer service over web like hotel bookings, resume creation, etc. 2. You can easily create your own events for evey page or action and track user experience on mixpanel that where are they mostly using and viewing on their site which helps in decision making to upgrade their products. 3. You can easily access the mixpanel data form anywhere just login to mixpanel site and you can see the realtime data. 4. It is also easy to implement on the source code of products . We just need to include one script and we are able to add events where ever we like to define.
cons: Sometimes we face issue in profiling of the users.

review_title: User analytics for the masses
description: Used at a couple of organizations now who have implemented it in very different ways. Both times I have been responsible for implementation. I feel like the 2nd time went better and that might be a testament to the UI.
author: Stuart P.
pros: Super flexible and simple to pick up, making user interaction analytics self-serve as much as possible
cons: The level of smart alerts were annoying - they should be dynamic. You can set a threshold but that threshold won't move based on a variable

review_title: Mixpanel gave us a tighter feedback loop on feature usage.
description: Mixpanel enabled us to easily test whether a new module we had developed was providing value, based on the actual activities of our users not only feedback forms. We tuned rollout strategies much more quickly than previously.
author: Vittal B.
pros: We use Mixpanel to slice and dice event data by user segments and to graph behavior trends over time. We used the retention reports to identify which features were associated with long-term engagement.
cons: The UI can become crowded with too many custom events, and it took some work to get consistent naming decisions across the team.

review_title: Mixpanel Review
description: Mixpanel is easy to use, functional and digestible.
author: Tom F.
pros: Easily digestible data and graphs. Easy to manipulate.
cons: Data can be slow to update, and is not real-time.

review_title: Great self-serve analytics tools that empower product teams
author: Andrei B.
pros: It has an intuitive interface that's great for myself and my team to be able to use when wanting to answer our own data questions without needing help from our product analytics team.
cons: There isn't a clear process for how to ensure the data and reporting options we use when self-serving data queries have been quality assured, and that the data and the way we interpret it is reliable

review_title: A powerful and intuitive tool to make the most of your data
author: María S.
pros: It is a very intuitive tool, and in just a matter of minutes, you can obtain fascinating graphs and results without a steep learning curve.
cons: It is important that your platform/website clearly defines the events you want to export in order to be able to exploit them later; this requires prior work.

review_title: Web analytics and customer engagement
author: Caleb O.
pros: The tool is easy to use and enhances customer engagement tracking and web analytics.
cons: No significant issues with the tool so far

review_title: MixPanel helps us to predict our business!
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: -Data and Metric prediction -Import/export -Ease of use and deployment -Nice interface and dashboard -Cost
cons: -Customer service isn’t as good as Adobe.

review_title: Powerful, comprehensive tool to track and analyze user actions and behavior patterns
description: It's a powerful analytics and marketing tool that helps you track and understand user behavior (who are your power users? which sources did you acquire them from? what's your churn rate? etc.) and make important business decisions about what you should do more of, what's working and what isn't.
author: Sapph L.
pros: Once you set it up properly, you can use Mixpanel to track every granular user action. You can save numerous details with each event that's important for you to know such as device, browser, country, campaign UTM, etc. Mixpanel provides you with several types of reports you can analyze data with such as Insights (break down user actions to show how many share X, Y or Z detail such as campaign UTM) and Funnels (conversions between user actions). You can also use Mixpanel to send notifications (email, push, in-app) to users based on their actions. It's a very powerful analytic and marketing tool.
cons: Lately, Mixpanel has made quite a few UI changes. Some have been helpful, others less so. Example with a recent funnels UI change: if you add a breakdown (which breaks down each of the funnel steps by a specific detail like country), Mixpanel adds several different colored bars to the funnel, which makes it very difficult to visually see the conversions between each step. I'm not sure if they do any testing with users before launching new features or UI but their user experience would benefit a lot from it. Mixpanel also does get expensive as you track more user actions (events), but it's only powerful and useful when you track every user action, big and small. Otherwise, your analysis would be based on an incomplete picture and cause incorrect findings.

review_title: A powerful tool for gaining real insights into users' behaviors.
description: With the support of Mixpanel, we are now able to gain a more in-depth comprehension of clients' behavior and generate reports and dashboards that provide a more precise comprehension of data.
author: Molsy K.
pros: Mixpanel is a great tool for tracking and analyzing how users act, and it is built in a way that makes it easy to get to the tools we need. I appreciate the ease of setting up different paths for data as well as having dashboards and reports that show all the details. With Mixpanel's collaboration features, I can get my team involved in all operations. This lets us mine data from more sources to get the best results.
cons: While I don't see this as a major issue, there are a few situations when we can't get to the necessary streams when setting up customized events.

review_title: Grow your online business by finding out caveats in your application
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Data analytics, event capturing, AB testing, customer support.
cons: Missed event are never captured and they just vanish so that needs to be addressed.

review_title: Data tool for more established companies
description: When working Mixpanel is fantastic. It can help companies see their acquisition, activation, retention, revenue and referral. But I think the product is primarily for more mature companies that have used queries and excel sheets in the beginning to make their initial KPI's. Don't use this from the get go as a startup. But use once you know exactly what you want to track, and get somebody on your team to be the super user of Mixpanel (data scientist) and do the same on the development team.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Mixpanel can tell you a lot of things about your users. Especially the Signal feature is great – we've used it as a guideline instead of having a full time data scientist. You can also follow different cohorts of users and create funnels to see where these users fall of or not.
cons: The dashboard function is not great. Besides that the system is pretty complex, so you really need to dive deep into the tutorials to understand how to use it.

review_title: Handling Analytics Made Easy
author: Catania G.
pros: Data analytics and analytics has been amazing. It is smooth too.
cons: It is powerful and helpful - no dislikes yet.

review_title: Basic analytics software for simple companies
description: Overall this product was easy to integrate and use, but ultimately did not provide the level of detail needed to run a successful data-driven business.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: This product provides easy-to-integrate code and some very basic metrics for your digital products. It is easy to use for beginners in data & analytics.
cons: There are many better visualizations and analytics metrics that could exist, but do not for the sake of simplicity. If you want to be more advanced in your ability to measure your data, look at other analytics platforms.

review_title: Mixpanel is an awesome tool for tracking users events
description: My overall experience with Mixpanel was great always been a go-to tool when comes to analysis and research.
author: Rajeesh N.
pros: Mixanel's Insights & Funnel feature is really helpful to understand users' activity on the product. It helped me improvise the UX of the dashboard and helped me with my research part too.
cons: The flow feature on the Mixpanel is something this I have used least and was a little useful. Mixpanel also doesn't fetches and creates events automatically, the developer has to create the event all the time.

review_title: MixPanel the best Software Analytics Tool
description: We use MixPanel to measure all the resources of our Software. With the collected data, we set up visualizations and dashboards to make decisions in our product, and in our sales proccess, because we use MixPanel to tracking our Free Trial. For Product Analytics, I do not doubt that the MixPanel is The best choice, even more, than we are a Startup and we have been counting on the startups program for some time.
author: Robson P.
pros: Mixpanel interface is excellent. For anyone who works with software as a service, this tool is essential! Distinguished product teams use this tool. MixPanel have too many resources, all you need when the subject is Product Analytics you can do with Mixpanel. I'm CTO on [SENSITIVE CONTENT HIDDEN] and use at least once on a week MixPanel to taking product decisions.
cons: The initial configuration of data tracking is a bit complicated, especially if you want to evaluate everything that is possible. You will take some time to setup the events, mainly the custom events of you product. Of course you need to evaluate the points the make sense to track, and if you love data like I, I'm sure that you will want to track everything.

review_title: Tracking user behavior is a must but we're still figuring out Mixpanel
description: We build a SaaS product and need some way of tracking and analyzing user behavior. Everyone talks about and recommends mixpanel, so we're trying to figure it out.
author: Maxim L.
pros: The new UI is really nice (visually), I love how graphs look like Mobile app is super snappy Integration is easy Automatic insights are actually useful
cons: Building useful graphs can be challenging and sometimes counter-intuitive. Of course, I could watch all their education videos, but I simply don't have enough time. I think there's a lot that can be done in the "getting started quickly" category. Also, I somehow can not pin a flow-diagram to the dashboard or even save it
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Day to day use of the software
author: Mohammed S.
pros: Ease and one stop for analysis and data management
cons: Sometimes the data is clustered. May have option to download csv

review_title: An Exceptional Analytic Tool
description: We have been tracking user events and Mixpanel has proved a great ally for that. It's easy to use features had made it stand out than other products in the market. I have been using this more for than six months now and not for once I faced any difficulties. It wasn't any arduous for me and I would suggest people who are looking for a great analytic tool to try this one.
author: Shrishtee S.
pros: Intelligible software it is, with user friendly interface. A new user doesn't have to be perturbed much to understand the functionality of this product. All the features are quite comprehensible.
cons: There is nothing in this product to dislike. The price might bother a few people but in the comparison to the other analytic software out there the price of this product is very much acceptable, making this product affordable than others.

review_title: So much data and So little time
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The ability to track all the different users on our system and truly analyze their journey within our product. As a Support Rep, I use mixpanel primarily to track users, see if they're able to function properly within our mobile app, so mixpanel is fantastic in this function and am sure provides much more to our team.
cons: The User interface within Mixpanel can be a lot for first time users. Even using the product for a couple of months now and I'm still not fully aware of how well to use it. I wish I had more time to figure out the different functions and learn the system more but that would be it's only drawback, that it's not for beginners.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Best analytics product there is but very expensive.
description: Great analytics but the pricing is no longer worth it.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Compared to others like Flurry and Amplitude there are much more features in Mixpanel. Lets you explore user profiles and see each users revenue and target push notifications to users with campaigns. Also lets you set properties on users so that you can enable features specific users and do A/B testing.
cons: The pricing is way to high for anyone other then a large company they used to have a good free plan but the current free plan with its limits is useless for production apps.
